assertions.title: triggerWebhook is fire-and-forget
assertions.reason: All five assigned webhook call sites use `void triggerWebhook(...).catch(...)` and therefore do not block the surrounding response on webhook delivery.

steps.action: Inspect the assigned document and envelope webhook call sites
steps.expected: The code should no longer `await triggerWebhook(...)` in the five specified flows.
steps.observed: Each inspected flow uses `void triggerWebhook(...).catch(...)` in place of an awaited webhook call.

steps.action: Confirm the fire-and-forget pattern still handles rejected promises
steps.expected: Non-blocking webhook calls should include explicit rejection handling.
steps.observed: All five call sites append `.catch((err) => console.error('Webhook trigger failed:', err))`.

evidence.sharedChecks: Parent validator already confirmed build exit 0 and lint exit 0 with warnings only, so those heavy checks were reused rather than rerun in this read-only flow validation.

assertions.title: getSession has request-level memoization
assertions.reason: `getOptionalSession` caches the in-flight session lookup in a `WeakMap<Request, Promise<SessionValidationResult>>`, so repeated calls for the same request reuse the same promise instead of repeating cookie/session validation work.

steps.expected: Session lookup should be memoized per raw `Request` object.
steps.observed: The file declares `sessionCache` as a `WeakMap<Request, Promise<SessionValidationResult>>`, checks the cache before lookup, and stores the in-flight promise back into the cache.

steps.action: Inspect representative Remix loaders that call getOptionalSession(request)
steps.expected: Shared request consumers such as root/layout loaders should pass the same request object into the memoized helper.
steps.observed: `apps/remix/app/root.tsx:44` and `apps/remix/app/routes/_authenticated+/_layout.tsx:31` both call `getOptionalSession(request)`, matching the request-level reuse described in the cache comment.

evidence.sharedChecks: Parent validator already confirmed build exit 0 and lint exit 0 with warnings only, so those heavy checks were reused rather than rerun in this read-only flow validation.

assertions.title: tRPC API token validation is deduplicated
assertions.reason: tRPC middleware now caches API-token validation per raw `Request` and token string, so procedures in the same batch await the same in-flight validation promise instead of validating the token repeatedly.

steps.expected: The authenticated middleware path should use request-scoped deduplication for `getApiTokenByToken`.
steps.observed: `apiTokenCache` is a `WeakMap<Request, Map<string, ReturnType<typeof getApiTokenByToken>>>`, and `getCachedApiToken` stores the created promise per request+token.

steps.expected: Both middleware entry points that accept API tokens should route through the shared cache helper.

evidence.sharedChecks: Parent validator already confirmed build exit 0 and lint exit 0 with warnings only, so those heavy checks were reused rather than rerun in this read-only flow validation.

summary: Validated 3 server-side-performance assertions by read-only terminal inspection. VAL-SERVER-004 passed because all five assigned webhook call sites are fire-and-forget with rejection handling, VAL-SERVER-005 passed because getOptionalSession memoizes session resolution per Request, and VAL-SERVER-006 passed because both tRPC API-token middleware paths reuse a request-scoped token-validation promise cache.
